question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause executeAsync(client) to return a CompletableFuture that never completes, and since Mono.fromFuture() does not cancel the underlying future when the Mono is cancelled (e.g., by timeout), the SDK continues holding the connection open, accumulating leaked futures proportional to the request rate?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a partial network partition (where some nodes are reachable and some are not) combined with the random client selection via secureRandom.nextInt() to cause non-deterministic publish failures, making it impossible for operators to distinguish a real network issue from a monitor misconfiguration?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes nodeSupplier.get() to return a node whose getAccountIds() returns an empty list, so that transaction.setNodeAccountIds() is called with an empty list, causing the SDK to throw an exception that bypasses the onErrorMap() check and propagates as a raw RuntimeException?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the NodeAddressBook.newBuilder().addAllNodeAddress(nodeAddresses).build().toByteString() serialization to produce a truncated or malformed byte string (due to partial data from a partitioned node), which is then passed to NodeAddressBook.fromBytes(), causing a silent parse error that results in an empty address book?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the flatMap chain clients.elementAt(clientIndex).flatMap(client -> getTransactionResponse(...).flatMap(r -> processTransactionResponse(...))) to hold references to both the Client and the in-flight TransactionResponse simultaneously, doubling the memory footprint per request during the partition window?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the .timeout(properties.getTimeout()) operator emit a TimeoutException on the Mono, but since the underlying gRPC call is still in-flight (not cancelled), the SDK retries internally, causing duplicate transaction submissions to the network after the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes nodeSupplier.refresh() to emit zero nodes, so that Flux.range(0, publishProperties.getClients()).flatMap(...) calls toClient() with an empty list, and toClient() builds a Client with an empty network map, which is then cached permanently, making the monitor unable to publish any transactions even after the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes client.setNodeMaxBackoff(publishProperties.getNodeMaxBackoff()) to be set to the default 1-minute value on a client that is already connected to a partitioned node, causing the SDK to back off for up to 1 minute before trying another node, effectively amplifying a short partition into a 1-minute outage per client?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause scenario.onNext() (called in doOnNext after successful publish) to never be called, while scenario.onError() is called for every failed request, causing the scenario's success counter to stagnate and error counter to grow, triggering false-positive alerts that mask the real partition event?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause Mono.fromCallable(() -> executable.execute(client)) to throw a checked exception that is wrapped by @SneakyThrows in toClient(), causing the exception type to be obscured and preventing proper error handling in the onErrorMap() operator?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the reactive pipeline to emit errors at a rate exceeding the downstream subscriber's demand, triggering back-pressure violations in the Mono chain and causing MissingBackpressureException to propagate, which is not handled by onErrorMap() and crashes the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition where a network partition occurs between nodeSupplier.refresh().collect(Collectors.toList()) completing and Flux.range(0, publishProperties.getClients()).flatMap(i -> Mono.defer(() -> Mono.just(toClient(nodes)))) executing, so that toClient() receives a node list that is already stale, building clients that immediately fail?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that occurs after a transaction is submitted but before the TransactionReceiptQuery completes, causing the receipt to be permanently lost, and since there is no retry for the receipt query, the transaction's outcome is never recorded in the scenario metrics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the TransactionRecordQuery with setQueryPayment(Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR)) to be submitted multiple times (due to SDK internal retries) during the partition, each time deducting 1 HBAR from the operator account, leading to uncontrolled fee drain?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the doOnNext(response -> log.info(...)) callback to never fire, while doOnError(scenario::onError) fires continuously, causing the log output to be flooded with error entries at the publish rate, exhausting disk space on the monitor host?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the nodes list passed to toClient() to contain duplicate AccountId strings, so that AccountId.fromString() is called multiple times for the same ID, and the resulting network map silently drops duplicates via (a, b) -> a, reducing the effective node count below the configured minimum?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes clients.elementAt(clientIndex) to emit an onError signal (if the cached Flux terminated with error), and since there is no fallback in the flatMap chain, the error propagates directly to onErrorMap(), wrapping it as a PublishException with the original request, potentially leaking sensitive transaction data in the exception message?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes nodeSupplier.get() to increment the AtomicLong counter without successfully routing a transaction, so that after partition recovery the counter has advanced far beyond the number of successful transactions, causing the round-robin node selection to skip nodes in a non-uniform pattern?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause executeAsync(client) to complete with a TransactionResponse that has a null or invalid transactionId, and since processTransactionResponse() uses transactionResponse.transactionId without null-checking, a NullPointerException is thrown that bypasses the onErrorMap() predicate check?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the Mono pipeline to be subscribed to but never complete (neither onNext nor onError), causing the subscription to leak and accumulate indefinitely, eventually exhausting the reactor scheduler's internal subscription tracking structures?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes client.setNetworkFromAddressBook() to throw a checked exception (wrapped by @SneakyThrows), which propagates out of toClient() as an unchecked exception, causing the entire getClients() Flux to terminate with an error that is then permanently cached, blocking all future publish operations?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the timeout operator to fire on a Mono that has already emitted a value (race condition between timeout and onNext), causing the downstream to receive both a value and a TimeoutException, leading to undefined behavior in the scenario metrics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes nodeSupplier.refresh() to hang indefinitely (due to the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...) in NodeSupplier), so that getClients() never completes, and the cached Flux<Client> never emits any elements, causing all clients.elementAt(clientIndex) calls to block forever?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause the secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients()) call to always select the same client index (due to PRNG state), concentrating all traffic on a single partitioned client while other healthy clients in the pool remain unused?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the execute() call for TransactionReceiptQuery to use a different Client than the one used for the original transaction submission, and since the receipt query client may point to a partitioned node, the receipt is never retrieved even though the transaction succeeded on a healthy node?
